commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves Markdown table alignment in shared Markdown IR, renders Telegram rich Markdown tables with bordered/striped and per-cell alignment attributes, updates focused Telegram tests, and bumps the plugin SDK text surface budget/type export.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +13, Other 0. Total +46 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source path: current main emits bare Markdown rich tables and does not preserve alignment metadata, while markdown-it exposes the missing alignment on table tokens. No live Telegram reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real Telegram proof and maintainer acceptance of the small SDK type surface before merge; there is no narrow code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(v2026-6-9): telegram richMessages breaks paragraph breaks and table rendering
target.body:
Related: #95554

## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix with focused regression coverage.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the diff is scoped to Telegram rich table rendering, shared Markdown table alignment metadata, and the plugin SDK surface budget required by the new public Markdown table alignment type export.
- Root cause: The root source of the regression is the shared Markdown IR parser boundary: it carried table rows and cells into the Telegram rich-message path but discarded Markdown separator-row alignment metadata, while the Telegram rich table renderer emitted a bare `<table>` tag without native table styling attributes.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch preserves the missing table alignment metadata at the shared Markdown IR source, emits it only when alignment information exists to keep unaligned metadata compatible, and renders that metadata in Telegram rich HTML table cells instead of patching a downstream string case.
- Patch quality notes: The change is limited to Telegram rich table formatting, focused Telegram tests, the shared Markdown table metadata type, the plugin SDK re-export needed by the existing Telegram renderer import boundary, and the matching plugin SDK surface budget update. The parser helper returns `undefined` for cells without a recognized markdown-it `text-align` style so unaligned tables keep the existing no-alignment contract rather than masking unknown table data with a default alignment.

Fixes an issue where Telegram users with `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` could receive Markdown tables without the expected rich-table visual structure. Rich Telegram tables now retain native bordered/striped table presentation and Markdown column alignment for both header and body cells.

## Linked context

Related: #95554

Root cause: the root source is the shared Markdown IR parser boundary, which carried table rows and cells into the Telegram rich-message path but did not retain Markdown separator-row alignment metadata; the Telegram rich table renderer then emitted a bare `<table>` tag. The fix carries alignment metadata only when a table has real aligned columns, preserving the existing unaligned Markdown table metadata shape, and renders Telegram rich tables with native `bordered` and `striped` attributes plus per-cell alignment.

Public contract / related open PR scan: the existing `channels.telegram.richMessages` public config contract is unchanged, and the related open PR / same-contract scan found no blocking canonical PR or public-contract competitor for this scoped Telegram table rendering fix. This does not change config defaults, schema validation, over-wide table fallback behavior, Telegram transport behavior, or non-Telegram channel rendering.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram rich-message Markdown tables now preserve rich table styling and Markdown column alignment when `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` is enabled.
- Real environment tested: focused repository tests for the shared Markdown IR, Telegram rich HTML renderer, Telegram rich send payload path, Telegram rich draft preview path, and plugin SDK surface reporting.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`; `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`; `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: all five focused Vitest commands completed with passing test files and passing assertions for the shared Markdown IR table metadata contract, Telegram rich HTML table rendering, Telegram send payload path, Telegram rich draft preview path, and plugin SDK surface budget.
- Observed result after fix: the Markdown IR contract test passed, Telegram rich formatting tests passed, Telegram send-path tests passed, the Telegram rich draft preview assertion passed, and the plugin SDK surface report test passed. The covered assertions verify that unaligned table metadata remains compatible, rich tables render as `<table bordered striped>`, Markdown alignment markers render matching cell `align` attributes, outbound `sendRichMessage` payloads and rich draft previews carry the bordered/striped table markup, and the public SDK surface budget matches the new exported table-alignment type.
- What was not tested: no change to Telegram Bot API transport behavior, channel config schema/defaults, non-Telegram renderers, or rich table support beyond the existing width limit.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: validation is focused on deterministic renderer, send payload, preview, metadata, and surface-report paths.

## Tests and validation

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed (1)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  59 passed (59)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  134 passed (134)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 132 skipped (133)
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  5 passed (5)
  ```

Regression coverage now verifies:

- Unaligned block-mode Markdown tables keep the existing metadata shape without an unnecessary `aligns` field.
- 20-column Telegram rich tables render as `<table bordered striped>` while over-wide tables still fall back to `<pre><code>`.
- Markdown alignment markers `:---`, `:---:`, and `---:` render matching `align="left"`, `align="center"`, and `align="right"` attributes on both header and body cells.
- The Telegram send path asserts that the outbound `sendRichMessage` payload carries the bordered/striped table markup.
- The Telegram rich draft preview path asserts that previews generated from Markdown tables carry the bordered/striped table markup.
- The plugin SDK surface report budget accounts for the new public Markdown table alignment type export.

## Risk checklist

- Why it matters / User impact: Telegram rich message recipients get structured Markdown tables that preserve borders, alternating row styling, and left/center/right column alignment when rich messages are enabled.
- What did NOT change: no change to Telegram Bot API transport behavior, channel config schema/defaults, non-Telegram renderers, or rich table support beyond the existing width limit.
- Public contract / related open PR scan: the existing `channels.telegram.richMessages` public config contract remains unchanged, and related open PR / same-contract scanning found no blocking canonical PR or public-contract competitor for this scoped Telegram table rendering fix.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: Markdown table alignment is captured at the shared Markdown IR source where markdown-it exposes separator-row alignment, then consumed by the Telegram rich renderer; unrecognized or absent alignment stays `undefined` so no default alignment is invented for unaligned tables.
- Target test file: `packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, and `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: unaligned block-mode table metadata stays compatible, aligned Markdown tables preserve left/center/right column alignment in Telegram rich HTML, rich tables request bordered/striped native presentation, both Telegram rich send payloads and rich draft previews carry the bordered/striped markup, and the SDK surface budget matches the new exported alignment type.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests cover the shared metadata contract, renderer output, outbound rich send payload, adjacent rich draft preview path, and CI-failing SDK surface budget without expanding the change into unrelated Telegram transport or config behavior.
- Risk labels considered: message-delivery and compatibility.
- Risk explanation: shared Markdown table metadata is used by plugin renderers, so the optional `aligns` field is emitted only when at least one table column has a real alignment value; unaligned table metadata remains compatible.
- Why acceptable: the diff is scoped to table metadata and Telegram rich table rendering, preserves public config/default behavior, keeps existing over-wide table fallback behavior, and is covered by focused regression tests.
- Highest-risk area: shared Markdown table metadata shape used by plugin renderers.
- Risk mitigation: `aligns` remains optional and is only emitted when at least one table column has a real alignment value, so unaligned block-mode table metadata remains compatible.
- Merge-risk labels: message-delivery, compatibility.
- Scope boundary: Telegram rich Markdown table rendering, shared Markdown table alignment metadata, and the matching SDK surface budget only.
- Out of scope: changing Telegram Bot API transport behavior, changing channel config schema/defaults, changing non-Telegram renderers, or expanding rich table support beyond the existing width limit.

## Current review state

Next action: ready for maintainer review after focused validation, CI-failure attribution, and passing ClawSweeper commit review.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users with `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` could receive Markdown tables without the expected rich-table visual structure. Rich Telegram tables now retain native bordered/striped table presentation and Markdown column alignment for both header and body cells.

## Why This Change Was Made

The rich Markdown table path now preserves Markdown table alignment metadata through the shared Markdown IR and renders Telegram rich tables with native `bordered` and `striped` table attributes. It keeps unaligned table metadata compatible by omitting the optional alignment field unless alignment information exists, and updates the plugin SDK surface budget for the new exported table alignment type.

## User Impact

Telegram rich message recipients get structured Markdown tables that preserve borders, alternating row styling, and left/center/right column alignment when rich messages are enabled. Existing unaligned Markdown table metadata remains compatible.

## Evidence

- `pnpm exec vitest run packages/markdown-core/src/ir.table-block.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed (1)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  59 passed (59)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  134 passed (134)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "renders rich draft previews only when enabled"`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 132 skipped (133)
  ```

- `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  5 passed (5)
  ```
